mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trajectory in real GDP growth compresses equity risk premiums and lifts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in quarters with positive GDPC1 growth are not statistically higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the federal funds rate signals aggressive monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25pp in a single month
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative when the trigger fires (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal produces positive returns in > 40% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong non-farm payroll growth and a declining unemployment rate confirm a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which boosts consumer spending and corporate revenue forecasts, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a two-month horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS changes and UNRATE declines yields an insignificant predictive coefficient (p > 0.10) over any 5-year rolling out-of-sample window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below a critical threshold during a supply-constrained expansion indicates bottleneck severity that erodes profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns when INDPRO drops below 95.0, or the cross-sectional predictive power (Rank IC) remains above 0.05 in the triggered subsample.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in housing starts reflects rising business and consumer confidence in future demand, which lifts construction sector earnings and broader market sentiment, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rolling 1-year Spearman correlation between HOUST month-over-month changes and 5-day SPX returns is consistently below 0.05 across all available data periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index crossing above a specific inflation threshold triggers repricing of long-duration equity cash flows and anticipates further rate hikes, depressing valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a two-month horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns do not decline significantly when CPI crosses the threshold, or the signal produces positive returns in more than half of all triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: The joint acceleration of real GDP growth and housing starts signals a synchronized demand boom that lifts multiple equity sectors simultaneously, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: A multivariate regression of 20-day SPX returns on the interaction of GDPC1 and HOUST changes yields an insignificant joint F-statistic (p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate during a late-cycle labor cooling phase signals a final labor market peak that precedes corporate earnings revisions downward, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal predicts positive returns when it should predict negative, or the out-of-sample Rank IC remains below 0.03 across three non-overlapping 2-year test periods.
